Index: app/test/accordion/page.tsx =================================================================== --- app/test/accordion/page.tsx +++ app/test/accordion/page.tsx @@ -0,0 +1,100 @@ +"use client"; + +import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion"; +import { useState } from "react"; + +const sampleItems: AccordionItem[] = [ + { + id: "item-1", + title: "What is this?", + description: + "This is a test page for the Accordion component. It demonstrates the component's functionality with sample content.", + }, + { + id: "item-2", + title: "How does it work?", + description: + "Click on the accordion headers to expand and collapse the content. The component supports smooth animations and keyboard navigation.", + }, + { + id: "item-3", + title: "Can I customize it?", + description: + "Yes! The Accordion component supports custom classNames for all its parts including root, item, trigger, icon, title, chevron, content, and description.", + }, + { + id: "item-4", + title: "Is it accessible?", + description: + "Absolutely. The component uses proper ARIA attributes including aria-expanded, aria-controls, and role='region' for screen reader support.", + }, +]; + +const disabledItems: AccordionItem[] = [ + { + id: "enabled-1", + title: "Enabled Item", + description: "This item can be expanded and collapsed.", + }, + { + id: "disabled-1", + title: "Disabled Item", + description: "This item cannot be interacted with.", + disabled: true, + }, + { + id: "enabled-2", + title: "Another Enabled Item", + description: "This item can also be expanded and collapsed.", + }, +]; + +export default function AccordionTestPage() { + const [value, setValue] = useState(null); + + return ( +
+

Accordion Test Page

Default Accordion

+ Click on the headers to expand/collapse. Only one item can be open at + a time. +

parts.text: The user wants to use the Accordion component for testing purposes. I should create a test page that demonstrates the Accordion component. Let me create a test route that shows the Accordion in action.
I'll create a test page for the Accordion component. Let me add it to the app directory.

The user wants to use the Accordion component for testing purposes. I've already created the directory structure for a test page. Now I'll create a page component that demonstrates the Accordion usage.

The user wants to use the Accordion component for testing. I've created a test page that demonstrates various states and configurations of the Accordion component. The page includes:

1. Default accordion with sample FAQ items
2. Controlled accordion with state management
3. Accordion with disabled items
4. Accordion with custom icons

The test page is accessible at `/test/accordion` and showcases the main features of the Accordion component.

Created a test page at `app/test/accordion/page.tsx` that demonstrates:

- **Default accordion** with sample FAQ items
- **Controlled accordion** with external state management  
- **Disabled items** showing interaction states
- **Custom icons** for each accordion item

Visit `/test/accordion` to see the Accordion component in action with different configurations.
